clinical_history: Elderly man complains of palpitations. The ECG is most consistent with which ONE of the following? (Note: V2 and V3 are likely reversed.)
diagnosis: The ECG shows classic multifocal atrial tachycardia (MAT). The effective ventricular rate at rest is quite fast (about 130) and irregular, with a narrow (normal) QRS complex duration. The rhythm simulates atrial fibrillation. But, note the true, discrete P waves, with at least three different morphologies occurring sequentially. In addition, there are non-specific ST-T changes and somewhat prominent left precordial R waves without definitive criteria for LVH. See Questions/Answers # 149 & 506 for comparison and further discussion.
